description:
Partial tokens at message boundaries (unclosed backticks, fenced code blocks, bold/italic markers, link syntax) are passed directly to the sanitizer and renderer, causing layout thrash as the DOM oscillates between error-recovery states.

**Fix:** Create a `sanitizeStreaming(raw: string): string` utility with a pre-pass that closes dangling markdown tokens before sanitization. Hook it into the streaming render path so the renderer always receives structurally valid (if truncated) markdown.

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/ava/src/utils/sanitizeStreaming.ts` — create: pure function, no React dependency
- `apps/ava/src/utils/sanitizeStreaming.test.ts` — create: unit tests for all dangling token patterns
- `apps/ava/src/components/chat/StreamingMessage.tsx` — apply `sanitizeStreaming` before passing to `<Markdown>`
- `apps/ava/src/hooks/useStreamContent.ts` — if sanitization lives in hook layer, apply here instead

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Unit tests cover: unclosed inline code, unclosed fenced block, unclosed bold, unclosed italic, unclosed link — all pass
- Snapshot test: 20 real-world streaming partial inputs parse to valid markdown AST with no parse errors
- No layout thrash observable in Chrome DevTools during a streamed message containing a code block
- Build passes, all tests pass

**Complexity:** medium
title: Add streaming-safe markdown sanitization
